explanation: The LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state. This is expressed by the implication in the chosen LTL template.  The antecedent of the implication checks for the conditions of standby and good sensor data, while the consequent checks for the autopilot being in the STANDBY state. The chosen template ensures that this implication holds globally (G).

The LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good, then in the next time step the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state. This property ensures that the autopilot will switch to the standby state only when the pilot is in control and the sensor data is valid. The chosen template ensures that the autopilot changes to STANDBY state in the next time step.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then in the next time step the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state from the MANEUVER state.  This is expressed by the implication within the globally operator: if the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good, then in the next time step the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state.  The temporal operator 'X' (next) is used to express the transition in the next time step. The formula also includes the assumption that the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (pullup) prior to the transition.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition from the "MANEUVER" state to the "STANDBY" state if the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good. It uses the "G" (globally) operator to ensure that this transition happens in all possible futures where the pilot is in control and sensor data is good. It also uses the "F" (eventually) operator to indicate that the transition will happen at some point in the future.

This LTL property states that globally, whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (pullup is true) and the pilot is in control (standby is true) and the sensor data is good (good is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY is true).

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (pullup is True) and the pilot is in control (standby is True) and sensor data is good (good is True), in the next time step the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY is True).

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the MANEUVER state to the STANDBY state when the pilot is in control and the sensor data is good. It does this by using the following conditions:

- **bool_exp2:** Checks if the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (pullup = True).
- **X bool_exp3:** Checks if the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state in the next time step (state_is_STANDBY = True).
- **bool_exp3:** Checks if the pilot is in control (standby = True) and sensor data is good (good = True).
- **G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)):** Globally ensures that whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state, it will transition to the STANDBY state in the next time step, provided the pilot is in control and the sensor data is good.

This property effectively ensures that the autopilot behaves as intended and transitions to the STANDBY state under the given conditions.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state in the next time step. The formula uses the implication operator (->) to express this conditional behavior, and the X operator to indicate that the transition occurs in the next time step.

The LTL formula ensures that whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from MANEUVER to STANDBY mode. The formula uses the "until" operator (U) to capture the notion of transitioning between states. It asserts that if the autopilot is currently in MANEUVER mode (pullup is True), then the state will eventually become STANDBY (state_is_STANDBY) before the pilot loses control (standby becomes False) or sensor data becomes bad (good becomes False). This guarantees that the autopilot will eventually transition to STANDBY mode under the given conditions.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) | (X ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))
